授業目的: 日本の祭りは、ただの遊びと娯楽なのか、あるいはより多様な意義を有するか。本授業では、全国で年間に開催される主要 な祭礼を通じて、日本文化を紹介する。授業目的は、これらの祭礼を、宗教的、文化的、社会的、経済的などの現象としてさまざまな視点から理解できることである。また、古代から日本における祭りと疫病の関連性、および祇園祭などのような重要な祭りの実施は２０２０年から２０２２年の間、パンデミックの状況に応じて、どのように適応されたかについても特に注目される。
到達目標:
Students will gain a comprehensive overview and understanding of 
Japanese religious and traditional rites and their place in contemporary
 society, as well as of the issues of gender, identity, and power. The students 
will also deepen their knowledge of the Japanese history and how it 
formed various festivals.
特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（伝統行事）（CIEC）/Religious and Traditional Rites（国際学部）
関連科目: There is no prerequisite for this course but a related course would be any standard course on Japanese religion and history.
授業の概要・背景: The course will explore the origins and significance of Japanese festivals, their role in preserving traditional Japanese values while also incorporating contemporary features and attracting tourists. We will pay particular attention to the elements of performance: rituals, processions, portable shrines, costumes, accessories, patterns of movement, music, food. Additionally, we will explore gender in the matsuri: display and affirmation of traditional ideals of femininity and masculinity, cross-dressing, gender fluidity, the body, models of gendered behavior and relations, the different roles of male and female community members, the increasing participation of women.

第1回:
Startup Week
Online Class (On-demand format) 《60 minutes》
Introduction. 
Link to Class material for Startup Week:
https://luna.kwansei.ac.jp/lms/contents?idnumber=2026464010000201#courseContent
30 minutes. Reading class materials for Core Week 1.
第2回:
Core Week 1
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Overview of  Japanese Cultural History
第3回:
Core Week 2
In-Person Class《90 minutes》
New Beginning - Oshôgatsu "New Year" 
Oshôgatsu - history, rituals, family bonds, special food. New Year in other Asian cultures.
第4回:
Core Week 3
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Setsubun "Change of Seasons": 
Setsubun "Driving out the Evil" - history, rituals - mamemaki, oni-barai (tsuina), commercialization and special food, performances: masks and costumes. Similar rites in other cultures.
第5回:
Core Week 4
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Matsuri and Gender:
The Girl's Day Festival - Hina Matsuri or Momo-no-sekku "Festival of the Peach." The Boy's Day Festival - Tango-no-sekku or Kodomo-no-hi "Children's Day." History, meaning, rituals, and art.
第6回:
Online Class (On-demand format) 《90 minutes》 May 15
Aoi Matsuri "Hollyhock Festival" and Jidai Matsuri "Festival of the Ages" (The Three Big Festivals in Kyoto):
Relation to Kyoto, Japanese history, and the imperial power. Elements of performance: processions, costumes, accessories, gendered bodies; display of ideals of traditional masculinity / femininity. Influence of Aoi Matsuri on Noh theatre.
Individual fieldtrip - Observation of Aoi Matsuri in Kyoto on May 15 (starts at 10:30~)
Students are requested to view class materials. Students can also observe in person Aoi Festival in Kyoto on May 15.
第7回:
Core Week 5
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Matsuri and Geinô "Festivals and Performance":
Types of performances, intergenerational bonding, initiation into manhood, gender, local identity. Some major examples - kabuki, noh, kagura, lion dances, puppets and marionettes.
第8回:
Core Week 6
In-Person Class《90 minutes》
Festivals in Okinawa and Amami Islands. Ainu Culture.
History and types, performances, gender aspects, local identities. Taketomi Island's Tanedori Festival and Amami Island Festival's sumo (shinji sumo "Sumo as Shinto Ritual"). Ainu festivals.
第9回:
Core Week 7
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Matsuri and Masculinity:
Hadaka Matsuri "Naked Festivals" and Kenka Matsuri "Fighting Festivals" - most popular examples, origins, meaning, rituals, display of the male body, homosociality and intergenerational bonding. Fertility festivals.
第10回:
Core Week 8
In-Person Class (90 minutes)
Buddhist Festivals:
Buddhism in Japanese society, influence on performance culture; Kanbutsu-e or Hana Matsuri "Buddha's Birthday"; Nehan-e "Buddha's Passing into Nirvana." Buddhist art. Buddhist festivals in other Asian cultures.
第11回:
Core Week 9
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Obon "Ancestors' Spirits Festival":
History, rituals, food, offerings, family bonds, bon odori "bon dances." Gozan Okuri-bi "Bonfires on Five Mountains" in Kyoto. Obon in Kyoto during the pandemic.
第12回:
Core Week 10
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Summer Festivals I:
Nagoshi-no-harae "Summer Purification Rites" and Tanabata "The Star Festival / The Lovers' Festival" - origins, meaning, changes in modern time. Special celebration in Kyoto during the pandemic.
第13回:
Core Week 11
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Summer Festivals II - Kyoto Gion Matsuri (The Three Big Festivals in Kyoto):
Origins and hostiry: relation to epidemic, significance in Kyoto culture and for local merchant class. Elements of performance: procession, costumes, accessories, art work, music, etc. Gender aspects: the all-male festival's "hyper-" and "stylized" masculinity, representation and recent inclusion of women. Gion matsuri during the pandemic. Influence of Gion matsuri, comparison with Osaka Tenjin Matsuri "Festival of Tenjin Deity."
第14回:
Core Week 12
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Student project presentations. Every student is required to do a short presentation on their final projects.
第15回:
Fieldtrip
In-Person Class 《120 minutes》
Fieldtrip to observe Gion Matsuri preparation (Date TBD - July 11 (Sat) or the first procession on July 17 (Fri).
トピック.授業外学習2:
Students are expected to do the assigned readings and watch the assigned video materials in advance of class, in order to take part in the discussions.
Attendance is mandatory. In the event of illness or other absence, please notify your instructor in advance of class.
Students are required to prepare one in-class 10-minute presentation on topic related to the class content, assigned by the instructor or offered by themselves (counts as class participation 40%). Students are also required to do a final presentation on their research project (60% of grade).
Students will be given extra credit for attending individually a festival and writing one-page response paper on it. They can do their in-class presentation on that festival.

教科書/Required texts0: None. Related reading and visual materials will be uploaded on LUNA in advance of class.
参考文献・資料/Reference books0:
Herbert Plutschow, "Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan", Abingdon, Oxon: Routledge, 2013 ISBN: 9781315073385
Helen Bauer & Sherwin Carlquist, "Japanese Festivals", Tokyo: Charles E. Tuttle, 1977 ISBN: 9784805303580

Additional readings and other handouts will be provided by the lecturer in advance.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: プレゼンテーション・発表／Presentation (07)
Originality, logical structure, research abilities, persuasive conclusion, presentation skills.
成績評価Grading2: 授業への参加度（自発性、積極性、主体性、等）／In-class participation,contribution (09)
Participation in discussions, in-class presentation content and originality.
成績評価Grading3: 備考　Note 																											Final Project Presentation (60%): Students are required to do additional research on a topicrelated to the class content and prepare a final presentation.Class Participation (40%): One in-class presentations of a festival or another topic related to the class-content, active participations in in-class discussions.
